meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Bridge the experience gap. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to build a high-impact portfolio as an AI builder.
content.answer_block: Stop building 'to-do' lists. To solve the no-portfolio problem, you must simulate a professional client workflow. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative feature development, you transform raw ideas into production-ready SaaS applications that demonstrate real-world engineering competence to potential employers.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a GitHub profile is the silent killer of junior careers. You have the skills, you have the ambition, but the lack of a tangible 'ship history' leaves you invisible to recruiters who prioritize proven execution over theoretical knowledge. The gap isn't your coding ability; it is your lack of a repeatable, professional delivery framework.

Today, we stop writing hobbyist code and start executing like a senior dev. By adopting a rigid client-facing workflow, you move from 'student developer' to 'product engineer.' We will use Antigravity to define your project architecture and Claude Code to accelerate your implementation, turning your first project into a showcase of professional-grade engineering.

steps.title: Define the Client Requirements
steps.description: Before writing code, document the user stories and technical constraints. Treat your project as if a high-paying client handed you a specification sheet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Create a comprehensive PRD for a [Project Name] SaaS. Include user flows, database schema, and API specifications. Focus on scalability and security.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a README.md outlining the project scope, technical stack, and core user stories defined in the PRD.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the skeleton of your application. This ensures your project structure follows industry best practices from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready project scaffold using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Implement a clean folder structure with separate directories for services, components, and hooks.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Break the project into atomic, testable features. Use Claude Code to write the logic for each service layer while maintaining strict separation of concerns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the authentication service layer using Supabase. Include error handling, loading states, and type-safe user sessions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install @supabase/supabase-js' && claude write 'Create auth service'

steps.title: Professional Testing Suite
steps.description: A portfolio project without tests is just a prototype. Implement unit tests for your critical path to demonstrate engineering maturity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest unit tests for the authentication service. Mock the Supabase client and cover edge cases for failed logins and expired tokens.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install --save-dev jest' && claude run 'npm test'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Ship your project to production and document the deployment process. A live URL is worth a thousand lines of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a CI/CD workflow file for GitHub Actions that runs tests and deploys to Vercel on push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always commit your work with descriptive, conventional-commit style messages to show you understand version control hygiene.
Include a 'Technical Decisions' document in your repo explaining why you chose specific libraries or architectures; this is what senior devs look for in interviews.
Use Claude Code to refactor your own code after finishing a feature; asking it to 'clean up for production' often reveals cleaner patterns you might have missed.
Never ship a project without a professional README that includes a high-level architecture diagram and a clear 'How to Run' section.
Treat your local development environment as a production sandbox; set up environment variables and secrets management from the very first day.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, record a 60-second Loom walkthrough of the app's functionality and embed it in your README. This visual proof combined with your code quality will put you in the top 1% of applicants.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the blueprint to transform from a student into a builder who ships real value. Stop waiting for permission to be a professional. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
